observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to retrospectively recompute the Road Network Account, ensuring the exclusion of residual value in the depreciation expense calculation; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary correcting entries to update the Road Network Account balances, including Depreciation Expense, Prior Period Adjustments, and Accumulated Depreciation Accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures regarding the nature and details of the prior period errors and the corresponding adjustments to net revenue, as reflected in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity for the two financial periods.

In light of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the HRMO to promptly update the salary details of employees in the GSIS portal, ensuring that no delays occur during periods of salary increments;
Advise the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to coordinate with GSIS for the reconciliation of unpaid premiums per individual account, and to secure the necessary supporting documents such as payroll records and service records in preparation for a face-to-face reconciliation; and
Confer with the GSIS management and concerned LGU employees to determine the most appropriate resolution for the unsettled premium balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Revisit its planning process, anticipating the current and future needs, to avoid unnecessary realignments and further delays in implementing development-driven PPAs; and
Direct the MPDC to regularly monitor PPAs for timely implementation; otherwise, propose the reprioritization of PPAs that are no longer viable for execution.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the end-users or implementing units of the Procuring Entity to formulate their respective Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities, and projects (PAPs); and
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to consolidated the PPMPs into the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as basis for all its procurement transaction as required under Section 7.2 of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the MDRRMO:
To conduct ocular inspection on the designated tree planting sites and provide the audit team of the Inspection Report together with pictures;
To provide the Audit Team: a) copy of donation papers from the Donee; b) information on the specific locations of the tree planting activities done in November and December 2024; and c) number of manpower and man-day required to complete the tasks for each site; and
For similar activities to be carried out in the future, formulate an Activity Design that would ensure success of the program
Enjoin the HRMO:
To draft a standard template for JO contracts that clearly outlines the duration of employment, terms and conditions, and specific duties and responsibilities in a specific job; and
To require the JOs to register in the biometric scan system for their daily time-in and -out.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
Enjoin the LDRRMO and the LDRRM Council to provide a more specific implementation timeline for PPAs;
Require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the implementation of PPAs under the LDRRMIP, incorporate the prior years’ unexpended LDRRM balances in the revised AIP, and aim for a financial delivery rate of at least 90% for planned PPAs in CY 2025; and
Instruct the LDRRMO to submit the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day following the end of each month.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Local Chief Executive:
Revise the existing contingency plans to exhaustively include resource gaps for materials and equipment; and
Enjoin the LDRRM Council to incorporate the resource gaps identified in the contingency plan into the LDRRMIP, thereby maximizing the local government’s disaster preparedness.

In light of the foregoing, we reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
Endorse the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to attend the training on the preparation and establishment of a GAD Database, and to subsequently take the lead in guiding the development and implementation of the said database; and
Instruct the GFPS to submit to the Office of the Auditor an electronic or paper copy of the GAD Database for the year 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Enjoin the Municipal Assessor to fast track the preparation of assessment roll per barangay based on the general revision of 2018;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which should be the basis of the accounting to set-up RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to set-up the correct RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2019, 2020 and 2021 and for the years thereafter, for proper presentation in the financial statements.
We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the RPT/SET Receivables and their related accounts and to immediately apply the correct accounting entries in recording RPT/SET collections in order to avoid subsequent consequential erroneous entries; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant monthly Summary of Collections (Provincial Form 60(a) and BLGF Form 2-a) in order to reconcile their recorded collections.

We recommended to the management to request from the DPWH a copy of the turn-over documents of the ‘Completion of Bonifacio Gym including Site Development’ and ‘Construction of MPB (Museum)’ and provide the Municipal Accountant with the turn-over documents for the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to record the buildings in the books of the LGU for proper presentation in the financial statements. Moreover, ensure to request for the turn-over documents on projects funded and implemented by other government agencies if the LGU shall have the control over the said asset.

We recommended to the management to:
Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account with its depositary bank {Landbank of the Philippines (LBP) or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), and inform the PCSO in writing of the details of the same (i.e. account name and number)};
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the total amount of ₱184,012.89 of LGU’s share from the sales of lotto/STL to ‘Trust Liability’ in the Trust Fund Books; and
Utilize the amounts received from PCSO for the Municipality’s health programs, medical assistance and charitable services, consistent with the PCSO Charter.

We recommended to the management to establish written guidelines on the receipt, distribution and reporting of donations in-kind received by the LGU to enhance transparency and accountability over the donations taking into consideration the centralization on the accounting of donated goods by vesting upon a single office or body the sole authority and responsibility relative to in-kind donations.
Management Comment:
The management commits to centralize their controls in relation to donations in-kind received rather than having every department adopting its own control measures in the receipt and distribution of donations in-kind.

We recommended to the management to require the contractor to immediately refund the ₱475,909.56 negative variance found in the technical review of the contract.
Management Comment:
The management promised to send a letter to the contractor demanding for the refund of the negative variance.

We recommended to the management to:
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording the liquidation reports of the barangays if such funds were utilized for purposes other than those for which the fund was intended; and
Require the barangay to submit a detailed budget proposal as an attachment to its request for financial assistance which shall then be used by the LGU in determining the reasonable amount of financial assistance to be granted to the barangay.
Management Comment:
With regards to the ₱10,000.00 financial assistance from LGU Bonifacio to Barangay Liloan, Kanao-Kanao, Dullan, Montol and Calolot which were used to purchase uniforms for Tanods and BHWs, the management commented that “It was being found out that there was a Resolution No. 539-S-2020 approving the Re-alignment of the said appropriations for the uniforms of respective CVOs of the 28 Barangays. This appropriation was done in connection with the Barangay’s Control Point on lock-downs due to the increasing case of COVID-19 during that time. However, we admit, we fail to carefully look-out for the revised MOA and failed to attach the said Resolution in the release of the fund. It was also being noticed that these financial assistances were from the general fund and not from the trust fund.”
Moreover, as to the financial assistance for the Implementation of Solid Waste Management, the said funds were from the trust fund and will communicate to the respective barangays on the matter.
The management assured that the audit observation will be duly observed on the next transaction that the LGU will have and the Accounting Office also commits to strictly examine the liquidation reports before recording them in the books.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Agriculturist to secure a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) from the Provincial Government of Misamis Occidental and amend if found to have been in conflict with the current practice of the LGU taking into consideration what is beneficial and favorable to both parties;
Enjoin the Municipal Agriculturist to fast track collection of the unpaid balances of the farmers to attain the sustainability of the program; and
Ensure that in the future agreements, the LGU shall retain a copy of the duly signed Memorandum of Agreement and furnish the same to the Local COA.
Management Comment:
The management already requested from the Provincial Government of Misamis Occidental a copy of the duly signed MOA and committed to act on other recommendations.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the BAC Secretariat to prepare a PPMP excel template that shall be filled out by different offices and consolidate the PPMPs to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) which shall be forwarded to the BAC for final recommendation of the appropriate procurement modality; and
Stop the practice of procuring common-use supplies and equipment separately by office and consider the procurement of such in a quarterly basis to conserve time and resources of the LGU and to avail of the best favorable price and terms in the procurement transactions, if ever such supplies and equipment are not available in PS-DBM.
Management Comment:
The management commented that they shall issue through an executive order to all offices to resubmit their existing PPMP format but with uniform pricelist and commits to prepare a google sheet for the PPMP for the ensuing year. Further, they shall no longer allow the procurement by office, in such case, the procurement transactions would be minimized, thus, they can save time, money and effort in their procurement processes.

We recommended to the management to:
Ensure that registration of all serviceable motor vehicles owned by the LGU be renewed in CY 2022 and for the years thereafter; and
Require the GSO to have an updated monitoring of all motor vehicles with information on the Certificate of Registration (CR) and Official Receipt (OR) to facilitate the timely renewal of registration with the LTO to avoid penalties for late payment and payment of damages for third party liabilities in case of accidents. Also, ensure that the GSO shall file a copy of the OR/CR.
Management comment:
The management concurs with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count over the LGU’s Property, Plant and Equipment. Physical count of PPEs should be done at least once a year and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor;
Direct the GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended to the management to enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of books at least quarterly and to timely recognize reconciling items in their respective books.

We recommended that the management should:
Refrain from granting of additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made;
Demand immediate settlement or liquidation of cash advances and/or cause the withholding of the amount due from the concerned officials and employees to compel them to settle their accounts. Management may resort to legal action if no settlement has been made after sending three (3) demand letters; and
File a request for authority to write-off for the accounts of the deceased personnel and for those accounts that are deemed dormant and liquidation is futile, to be guided by the procedures contained in COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the management:
Prepare a revised billing statement to the lessee imposing the rates prescribed in the Municipal Revenue Code of 2016;
Install a billing system with appropriate internal control wherein the income and receivables shall be timely recognized; and
Revisit the pro-forma lease agreement and consider amendments on above-noted deficiencies with due consideration on a fair and reasonable provisions.

We recommended that the management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all fund releases to DCSPGs and liquidations using the following entries:

Upon release to DCSPG:
Due from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations	   xxx
Cash in Bank, LCCA					xxx
Upon liquidation:
Due to NGAs					  xxx
Due from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations	     xxx
Require the MSWDO to ensure that liquidation reports received from DCSPG shall be duly supported with complete supporting documents and shall be forwarded to the Accounting Office for the recording of the liquidation.
We have recommended to management to require the concerned claimants to provide legal basis otherwise refund the amount invalidly claimed.

In order to ensure that the utilization of 20% Development Fund are for purposes for which the fund is intended, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and all officials responsible in the preparation of the budget and utilization of funds should observe strictly the guidelines set forth under DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in identifying the programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the management require the BAC to prepare and submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to GPPB in printed and electronic format every semester as required in Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Office along with the GAD Focal Point System to establish GAD Database for storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy information.
